name: Hot-And-Spicy Short Ribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the sesame seeds in a pie tin at 300°F for 12-to-16 minutes, stirring often until golden.
2. Score the meat on each short rib with a crisscross pattern, making cuts about 3/8-inch apart and cutting almost to the bone.
3. Place the short ribs in a large shallow baking pan. Combine the remaining ingredients (except sesame seeds) and pour the marinade over the meat, tossing well to coat.
4. Cover and refrigerate for 24 hours, turning the ribs occasionally in the marinade.
5. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Arrange the ribs on a rack in a shallow roasting pan and brush with some marinade.
6. Roast uncovered for 20 minutes.
7. Remove the pan from the oven, turn the ribs, and brush with more marinade. Raise the oven temperature to 500°F.
8. After standing for 10 minutes at room temperature, return the ribs to the oven and roast for 5 minutes.
9. Turn the ribs again, basting generously with the remaining marinade, and roast until desired doneness (rare: 8 minutes; medium-rare: about 8 minutes).
10. Serve with fluffy boiled rice and a crisp salad. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
